summary: Older adults have an inherently high risk for falling due to age-related declines such as muscle loss, poor balance, reduced senses, and polypharmacy. This heightened vulnerability persists even among older adults diagnosed with cancer, where these underlying factors remain significant contributors to fall incidence.
